assertions.reason: As Org A owner, GET /api/persons returned only the Org A fixture and direct access to the known Org B person ID returned 404.
As Org A owner, GET /api/staff returned only the Org A staff fixture and direct access to the known Org B staff ID returned 404.
As Org A owner, GET /api/care-plans returned only the Org A plan fixture and direct access to the known Org B plan ID returned 404.
Org B list queries for persons and care plans showed only Org B data, but the required /api/care-notes route is missing and returned a framework 404 page instead of a tenant-scoped API response.
The direct viewer bypass attempt against PUT /api/persons/:id returned 405 Method Not Allowed because no PUT handler exists, not the expected 403 permission denial.

A real viewer session token was modified from role=viewer to role=owner and then invalidated; the protected API did not return 401 and instead redirected to /login with 302 while clearing the session cookie.

frictions.description: To obtain org-scoped API sessions, I had to use the Auth.js credentials callback flow directly; the user-testing guidance does not mention that the custom /api/auth/login path does not populate activeOrgId/role for API authorization.

notes: All raw curl outputs and HTTP status codes are captured in the evidence files listed above.

Seeded isolation fixtures were limited to tenantiso-org-a-20260401 and tenantiso-org-b-20260401 using tenantiso-owner-a-20260401@example.test, tenantiso-owner-b-20260401@example.test, and tenantiso-viewer-20260401@example.test.
